evidence_quotes.quote: video games face the unique risk of being entirely erased

paragraphs.text: I am providing feedback with a heartfelt suggestion to include a clause regarding the protection of video games after they are sold. This proposal is thoughtfully aligned with the "Stop Destroying Videogames" European Citizens' Initiative. The core concern is the fragility of video games, which can become unplayable once developers withdraw support/feel like it. Unlike other forms of media that endure, video games face the unique risk of being entirely erased, leading to cultural and creative loss for both consumers and society at large. To address this, I propose that the initiative requires developers/publishers to establish a clear end-of-life or support plan for video games. This plan should ensure the continued playability of games, even after official support concludes. Things such as: - Requiring mechanisms for archival and preservation, allowing these digital assets to be accessed by future generations. - Upholding consumer rights, ensuring that individuals retain the ability to access and enjoy games they have legally acquired.
